summary: Dashboard app with multiple panels: Gas Refill dates, Prayer Times (Shafi, 12-hour format), Weather (temperature + rain probability), Working Days counter (MAM PMC, countdown to 30th May excluding Sundays), and a new Milk Packet Delivery section backed by Supabase (MilkDB).
architectural_notes: Supabase integration: project https://ppebfdrluntbglqlreuz.supabase.co, MilkDB table with columns Date (PK), MilkDelivered (Yes/No), Packets (number)
known_issues: Displayed Muslim Shafi prayer timings are incorrect; needs recheck/verification.
NetworkError when attempting to fetch MilkDB records for Excel download — fetch from Supabase fails at download time
MilkDB record insertion (previously working) now also fails with NetworkError — both insert and fetch from Supabase MilkDB are broken
Supabase REST API returning 'invalid API key' error — anon key may be malformed or missing in request headers

existing_features.title:
Update the "Refill Delivered On" section in `frontend/src/components/GasRefillPanel.tsx` to display exactly this content:

"Refill Delivered On Below Dates:"
- "8th Dec 2025"
- "21st Jan 2026"
- "3rd Feb 2026"

Replace the current "Previously on" / "Latest on" layout for this section with a simple list of the three dates under the new heading text, keeping the existing card styling consistent with the rest of the panel.
Update `frontend/src/components/PrayerTimesPanel.tsx` so that displayed prayer times are shown in 12-hour format with AM/PM (e.g., "5:12 AM") instead of the raw API time string.

Simplify `frontend/src/components/WeatherPanel.tsx` to show only (1) the current temperature and (2) the rain possibility percentage for the current date, removing the 24-hour hourly list and the Solar Times section from the UI.

Recheck and correct the Aladhan API request in `frontend/src/components/PrayerTimesPanel.tsx` so that the displayed prayer times are actually computed using Shafi settings (not Hanafi), and ensure the UI label reflects the calculation configuration being used.

Add minimal in-UI transparency for verification by showing the calculation settings being used (e.g., method identifier and school/madhab) in the Prayer Times panel subtitle or footer, using English text.

In the frontend UI, replace every occurrence of the label 'DJF PMC' with 'MAM PMC'. This change should be applied wherever the text 'DJF PMC' appears across all frontend components (most likely in App.tsx or the working-days counter panel).

Calculate and display the number of days remaining from today's date to 31st May (of the current or next applicable year), excluding Sundays from the count. Show this count in the UI alongside or within the working-days counter panel, clearly labelled (e.g., 'Days to 31st May (excl. Sundays): X').

In `frontend/src/App.tsx`, update the countdown target date from 31st May to 30th May for the 'Days to 30th May (excl. Sundays)' counter. The label in the UI should also be updated to reflect '30th May' instead of '31st May'. Ensure the calculation still excludes Sundays.

Create a new frontend component `frontend/src/components/MilkDeliveryPanel.tsx` with the heading 'Milk Packet Delivery Details'. It must include: (1) a date input field, (2) a 'Milk Delivered' selector with options 'Yes' and 'No', and (3) a numeric input for 'Number of Packets'. On form submission, insert a record into the Supabase table `MilkDB` (project URL: https://ppebfdrluntbglqlreuz.supabase.co, anon key: eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZSIsInJlZiI6InBwZWJmZHJsdW50YmdscWxyZXV6Iiwicm9sZSI6ImFub24iLCJpYXQiOjE3NzI0MjM1OTMsImV4cCI6MjA4Nzk5OTU5M30.3uk-txeUkdVxcPtAQ6lM58Px2rlpdqQTxVEter4Y_nA) with columns: `Date` (primary key, date string), `MilkDelivered` (text: 'Yes' or 'No'), `Packets` (number). Use the Supabase JS client (`@supabase/supabase-js`) configured with the provided URL and anon key. Display success/error feedback to the user after submission.

Below the input form in `MilkDeliveryPanel.tsx`, add a button labelled 'Download as Excel' that fetches all records from the Supabase `MilkDB` table and exports them as an Excel (.xlsx) file using a client-side library (e.g., `xlsx` / SheetJS). The downloaded file should contain columns: Date, MilkDelivered, Packets.
Integrate `MilkDeliveryPanel` into `frontend/src/App.tsx` so it is rendered in the main dashboard layout, consistent with the existing panel grid/styling.

In `frontend/src/App.tsx`, remove the 'Target: Feb 28, 2026' label and any associated 'days to go' countdown display that references Feb 28. These UI elements must no longer appear anywhere in the rendered dashboard.

Fix the Supabase client initialization in `frontend/src/lib/supabase.ts` so that inserting and fetching records from the MilkDB table no longer throws `SecurityError: LockManager.request is not allowed in this context`. The root cause is that the Supabase JS client (loaded from CDN) internally attempts to use the Web Locks API (`LockManager`), which is blocked in certain browser contexts (e.g., ICP-hosted frontends). Replace the CDN-loaded Supabase client with the npm package `@supabase/supabase-js` (if not already installed), and configure it with `auth: { persistSession: false, autoRefreshToken: false, detectSessionInUrl: false }` to prevent it from accessing browser APIs that are unavailable in this environment. Ensure the `insertMilkRecord` and `fetchMilkRecords` functions continue to work correctly after this change.

Rewrite `frontend/src/lib/supabase.ts` so that both `insertMilkRecord` and `fetchAllMilkRecords` use native `fetch` calls against the Supabase REST API (project URL: https://ppebfdrluntbglqlreuz.supabase.co, anon key: eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZSIsInJlZiI6InBwZWJmZHJsdW50YmdscWxyZXV6Iiwicm9sZSI6ImFub24iLCJpYXQiOjE3NzI0MjM1OTMsImV4cCI6MjA4Nzk5OTU5M30.3uk-txeUkdVxcPtAQ6lM58Px2rlpdqQTxVEter4Y_nA) with all required headers set on every request: `apikey: <anon-key>`, `Authorization: Bearer <anon-key>`, `Content-Type: application/json`, and `Prefer: return=minimal` (for inserts). The insert endpoint must be `POST https://ppebfdrluntbglqlreuz.supabase.co/rest/v1/MilkDB` with the record body serialised as JSON. The fetch endpoint must be `GET https://ppebfdrluntbglqlreuz.supabase.co/rest/v1/MilkDB?select=*`. Do not use the `@supabase/supabase-js` package — keep the implementation as a pure native-fetch REST client to avoid any SDK-level browser API dependencies (LockManager, IndexedDB, etc.). Both functions must resolve successfully and throw/return a descriptive error string on failure.

Audit and fix `frontend/src/lib/supabase.ts` so that every native `fetch` call to the Supabase REST API sends the anon key correctly. Ensure the `apikey` header and `Authorization: Bearer <anon-key>` header are both set verbatim using the exact anon key string: `eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZSIsInJlZiI6InBwZWJmZHJsdW50YmdscWxyZXV6Iiwicm9sZSI6ImFub24iLCJpYXQiOjE3NzI0MjM1OTMsImV4cCI6MjA4Nzk5OTU5M30.3uk-txeUkdVxcPtAQ6lM58Px2rlpdqQTxVEter4Y_nA`. The key must not be truncated, URL-encoded, split across lines, or modified in any way. Both `insertMilkRecord` (POST to `https://ppebfdrluntbglqlreuz.supabase.co/rest/v1/MilkDB`) and `fetchAllMilkRecords` (GET to `https://ppebfdrluntbglqlreuz.supabase.co/rest/v1/MilkDB?select=*`) must include these headers on every request. Add `Content-Type: application/json` and `Prefer: return=minimal` for insert requests. After the fix, neither call should return a 401 'invalid API key' response from Supabase.

Rewrite `frontend/src/lib/supabase.ts` completely from scratch as a pure native-fetch REST client. Both `insertMilkRecord` and `fetchAllMilkRecords` must use `window.fetch` (or global `fetch`) with the following headers set verbatim on every single request — the anon key string must appear as a single unbroken literal in the source code and must not be split, concatenated, stored in an intermediate variable that could be truncated, or modified in any way:

- `apikey`: `eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZSIsInJlZiI6InBwZWJmZHJsdW50YmdscWxyZXV6Iiwicm9sZSI6ImFub24iLCJpYXQiOjE3NzI0MjM1OTMsImV4cCI6MjA4Nzk5OTU5M30.3uk-txeUkdVxcPtAQ6lM58Px2rlpdqQTxVEter4Y_nA`
- `Authorization`: `Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZSIsInJlZiI6InBwZWJmZHJsdW50YmdscWxyZXV6Iiwicm9sZSI6ImFub24iLCJpYXQiOjE3NzI0MjM1OTMsImV4cCI6MjA4Nzk5OTU5M30.3uk-txeUkdVxcPtAQ6lM58Px2rlpdqQTxVEter4Y_nA`
- `Content-Type`: `application/json` (all requests)
- `Prefer`: `return=minimal` (insert requests only)

Endpoints:
- Insert: `POST https://ppebfdrluntbglqlreuz.supabase.co/rest/v1/MilkDB` with the record body as JSON.
- Fetch all: `GET https://ppebfdrluntbglqlreuz.supabase.co/rest/v1/MilkDB?select=*`

Do NOT use `@supabase/supabase-js` or any Supabase SDK. Do NOT use XMLHttpRequest. Both functions must return the resolved data on success and throw (or return) a descriptive error string on failure. The file must export `insertMilkRecord` and `fetchAllMilkRecords` so that `MilkDeliveryPanel.tsx` continues to work without changes.

Rewrite `frontend/src/lib/supabase.ts` to fix the persistent 'Failed to fetch' / NetworkError affecting both `insertMilkRecord` and `fetchAllMilkRecords`. The root cause is likely a CORS or request-construction issue when calling the Supabase REST API from an ICP-hosted frontend. Apply the following fixes: (1) Use the global `fetch` (not `window.fetch`) to make all requests. (2) Add `mode: 'cors'` and `credentials: 'omit'` explicitly to every fetch call's options object. (3) Ensure all headers are set as a plain object literal — never as a `Headers` instance — with the exact anon key `eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZSIsInJlZiI6InBwZWJmZHJsdW50YmdscWxyZXV6Iiwicm9sZSI6ImFub24iLCJpYXQiOjE3NzI0MjM1OTMsImV4cCI6MjA4Nzk5OTU5M30.3uk-txeUkdVxcPtAQ6lM58Px2rlpdqQTxVEter4Y_nA` set verbatim in both `apikey` and `Authorization: Bearer <key>` headers on every request. (4) The insert endpoint is `POST https://ppebfdrluntbglqlreuz.supabase.co/rest/v1/MilkDB` with headers `Content-Type: application/json` and `Prefer: return=minimal` in addition to auth headers. (5) The fetch endpoint is `GET https://ppebfdrluntbglqlreuz.supabase.co/rest/v1/MilkDB?select=*` with auth headers only. (6) Both functions must throw a descriptive error string on non-OK HTTP status or network failure. (7) Do NOT use `@supabase/supabase-js` or any Supabase SDK. Do NOT use XMLHttpRequest. The file must continue to export `insertMilkRecord` and `fetchAllMilkRecords` so that `MilkDeliveryPanel.tsx` requires no changes.

Update `frontend/src/components/GasRefillPanel.tsx` to display the 'Cooking Gas Changed On' section with the following layout: a 'Previously on:' sub-label listing '24th Dec 2025' and '26th Jan 2026', and a 'Latest on:' sub-label showing '2nd Mar 2026'. Additionally, update the 'Refill Delivered On Below Dates:' section to use the same 'Previously on:' / 'Latest on:' layout: 'Previously on:' listing '8th Dec 2025' and '21st Jan 2026', and 'Latest on:' showing '3rd Feb 2026'. Keep all existing card styling consistent with the rest of the panel.

new_feature_requests.summary: Change prayer timings display to 12-hour AM/PM format
Reduce weather section to show only current temperature and rain possibility percentage for the date
Change 'DJF PMC' label to 'MAM PMC' in the UI
Calculate and display the number of days to 31st May excluding Sundays
Change countdown target date from 31st May to 30th May (Saturday)
Add 'Milk Packet Delivery Details' section with date input, milk delivered (Yes/No), number of packets fields stored in Supabase MilkDB, and a button to download the table as Excel
Remove 'Target: Feb 28, 2026' label and the 'days to Feb 28' countdown from the UI
Fix SecurityError: LockManager.request not allowed in context — Supabase MilkDB entry submission broken
Fix NetworkError when fetching MilkDB records for Excel download

Fix the `fetchAllMilkRecords` function in `frontend/src/lib/supabase.ts` so that fetching all records from the Supabase `MilkDB` table no longer throws a NetworkError. The current implementation uses native `fetch` directly against the Supabase REST endpoint. Update the fetch call to ensure correct headers are set (including `apikey` and `Authorization: Bearer <anon-key>` headers) and that the REST URL is properly formed (e.g., `https://ppebfdrluntbglqlreuz.supabase.co/rest/v1/MilkDB?select=*`). After the fix, the 'Download as Excel' button in `MilkDeliveryPanel.tsx` must successfully retrieve all rows and export them as a `.xlsx` file using SheetJS.
Fix 'invalid API key' error thrown by Supabase REST calls in MilkDeliveryPanel — likely an issue with how the anon key is passed in headers

Update GasRefillPanel to show two sections: 'Cooking Gas Changed On' (Previously: 24th Dec 2025, 26th Jan 2026; Latest: 2nd Mar 2026) and 'Refill Delivered On Below Dates' (Previously: 8th Dec 2025, 21st Jan 2026; Latest: 3rd Feb 2026)

projectName: Update Gas refill section to show delivered refill dates list
